verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of proteins. IZUMO1 is the founding member, a single-pass TM protein on sperm essential for gamete fusion. IZUMO4 (also called IZUMO sperm-egg fusion protein 4) retains the IZUMO domain but its exact membrane topology is less clearly established — some analyses suggest it is a GPI-anchored or secreted form rather than a classical single-pass TM protein. Regardless of anchor mechanism, IZUMO family members are expressed in a highly tissue-restricted manner, predominantly in testis/sperm. Any surface display is therefore restricted to germline/reproductive lineage cells (sperm or early embryo contexts). Under the triage rules, even if IZUMO4 has its own anchor in that compartment, expression restricted to a narrow germline/developmental lineage maps to `contextual` / `tissue_restricted_surface`. There is no documented surface pool in somatic cancer cells, immune cells, or stress conditions. No clinical surface-directed therapeutic programs are known for IZUMO4.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Exact membrane topology of IZUMO4 (GPI-anchored, TM, or secreted) is not definitively established in the literature; its surface display in sperm specifically needs confirmation.
